content.text:
Heat changes materials! When we heat something up or cool it down, amazing things can happen.

🔥 Heating makes things change:
• Ice → Water (melting)
• Water → Steam (evaporating)
• Bread dough → Bread (baking — this one can't go back!)
• Butter → Melted butter (melting)

❄️ Cooling makes things change:
• Water → Ice (freezing)
• Steam → Water (condensing)
• Melted chocolate → Solid chocolate (solidifying)

↩️ Reversible vs Irreversible Changes:
Some changes can be undone — ice melts to water, and water can freeze back to ice. These are REVERSIBLE changes.

Some changes cannot be undone — once you bake a cake or burn wood, you cannot get the original material back. These are IRREVERSIBLE changes.

Temperature is how hot or cold something is. We measure temperature with a thermometer. Water freezes at 0°C (32°F) and boils at 100°C (212°F).

Knowing about heating and cooling helps us cook food, make ice cream, and keep our homes warm!

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing reversible and irreversible changes with heat

experiment.instructions: Heat the butter by dragging it onto the hot pan! Watch what happens when heat is applied.

combinations.resultLabel: The solid butter melts into liquid! Heat causes the butter to change from solid to liquid. This is a reversible change — cool it down and it becomes solid again!

practiceQuestions.prompt: What happens to water when it is cooled to 0°C?
practiceQuestions.hint: Think about how ice forms in a freezer — what does cold do to water?

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which of these is an IRREVERSIBLE change?
practiceQuestions.hint: An irreversible change cannot be undone. Can you turn a baked cake back into raw batter?

practiceQuestions.prompt: What instrument do we use to measure temperature?
practiceQuestions.hint: Think about the tool a doctor uses to check if you have a fever.

questions.prompt: When water turns to steam, this is called:
At what temperature does water freeze?
What happens when steam is cooled?
Why is baking a cake an irreversible change?

options.text: Because you cannot turn the baked cake back into raw batter
